description_long: This object is a vintage-style corded telephone with a rectangular base and a metallic finish. The base is boxy and features a rotary dial on its top surface, with a metallic cradle supporting the handset above. The handset is cylindrical, with rounded earpiece and mouthpiece sections, and is connected to the base by a coiled cord. The metallic components have a brushed, slightly glossy appearance, while the base panels are lighter in color, possibly painted or covered with a textured material. The rotary dial is centrally positioned, and the cradle arms are symmetrically mounted on the base. The overall design evokes early 20th-century telephones, blending utilitarian and decorative elements.
description: A vintage metallic corded telephone with a rectangular base, rotary dial, and a coiled cord connecting the handset to the cradle. The design features a brushed metal finish and classic rotary interface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, metallic cradle, and both earpiece and mouthpiece of the handset clearly.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the base, the profile of the handset, and the coiled cord extending from the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the cradle, the base, and the coiled cord attachment point.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handset profile and the rotary dial from an angle, with the coiled cord more visible.
